raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Citibank N.A., my credit card issuer, regarding their mishandling of a dispute I filed for a charge of {$98.00} on XX/XX/XXXX from a foreign merchant ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ). 

This was an unauthorized annual renewal for a digital subscription I no longer used. I had deleted the app before the renewal and received no notice that I would be charged. After I was billed, I promptly contacted the merchant on XX/XX/XXXX to cancel and request a refund, but they refused. I then disputed the charge with Citibank. 

Citibank denied my dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, siding with the merchant but at no point did Citi ever invite me to submit my own documentation or evidence. 

Citi failed to conduct a reasonable investigation as required by 15 U.S.C. 1666 ( a ) of the Fair Credit Billing Act. I was never contacted or invited to submit my own documentation, even though the merchant submitted a XXXX rebuttal. Citi accepted the merchants position without evaluating my side or applying Mastercards rules for recurring billing. 

This is not just a customer service issue it reflects both merchant misconduct and issuer failure : The merchant ( XXXX ) admits in writing that they do not send pre-renewal reminders, which is a direct violation of XXXX Transaction Processing Rules ( Section 5.4.1 ) for subscriptions billed on cycles longer than XXXX months. 

Citi accepted this merchant response without asking for my rebuttal and closed the case based solely on the merchant 's terms of service. 

Citis process denied me due process as a consumer and failed to enforce XXXX  standards for recurring billing.
